title: Growth Manager

Discuss understanding of Indian advertiser segments — from informal SMBs to large brands. Show understanding of Google's advertising ecosystem and India-specific growth opportunities.

• 5 years experience in digital advertising, consultative sales, business development, or digital marketing

title: Product Manager, Google Pay

His understanding of how Indian SMBs actually conduct business (voice-led WhatsApp operations) can inform Google Pay's merchant product strategy. Lead with: (1) Dahod bakery story — understanding how Indian SMBs actually use digital tools, (2) 2.3% revenue uplift from fixing enforcement for 10M businesses, (3) Shoppertainment as commerce innovation, (4) Field research methodology for understanding payment behaviours in Tier 2/3 India. Privacy-centric measurement is a strategic area where his understanding of advertiser needs and user behaviour could add value. However, this requires people management experience (2 years direct reports) and deep technical measurement expertise, which are gaps.
how_to_win: Frame advertising products work at Meta as measurement-adjacent — understanding how advertisers measure ROI, what metrics matter for Indian SMBs. Discuss privacy considerations from the SMB enforcement work (balancing enforcement with business impact). Be transparent about people management gap. This is a stretch role — only pursue if other top-fit roles don't pan out.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 8 years product management or related technical role
• 5 years taking technical products from conception to launch
• 2 years people management with direct reports and technical leadership

title: AI Sales Specialist
role_summary: AI sales specialist driving customer adoption of AI solutions and delivering business growth, building client relationships in the AI technology space.
why_good_fit: AI-focused role in India — hits the AI-native criteria. Jay's AI fluency, understanding of AI adoption patterns in India (enterprise, SMB, consumer segments), and ability to articulate AI value propositions are relevant. His MetaAI field work gives real insight into how Indian users adopt AI. However, this is a sales role, not product — Jay's experience is more product-side.
how_to_win: Frame AI expertise as sales-enabling: understanding customer AI adoption patterns, ability to articulate value propositions based on real user research (20+ AI interviews). Discuss AI adoption barriers and opportunities in India. Lead with AI fluency credentials. Be transparent that this is a career pivot from product to sales — position it as bringing product insight to the sales conversation.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 4 years sales, client relationships, or account management
• Experience in business development driving AI solution adoption

title: Account Manager, Workspace, SMB (English)
role_summary: Account manager for Google Workspace targeting SMB customers, building relationships and growing spend among small and medium businesses.
why_good_fit: SMB focus directly aligns with Jay's deepest expertise — understanding Indian SMBs, their digital adoption patterns, and their business needs. His 10M businesses impacted at Meta and deep field research with Indian SMBs are highly relevant. However, this is an 'Early' level sales/account management role, which is significantly below Jay's seniority and is more sales than product.
how_to_win: Only pursue if other roles don't materialise. Lead with SMB expertise: understanding how Indian SMBs adopt technology, what drives their purchasing decisions, the voice-led WhatsApp-first operating model. Frame as someone who can bring unprecedented SMB insight to the account management function. The seniority mismatch is the main concern.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 2 years sales, client relationships, or account management
• Experience in technology sales
• English fluency

title: Senior Strategist, Trust and Safety, Shopping
role_summary: Senior strategist for Trust and Safety in Google Shopping, using data analytics to develop and implement safety strategies for the shopping platform.
why_good_fit: Shopping + Trust & Safety echoes Jay's SMB enforcement work at Meta — he identified how enforcement policies were harming legitimate Indian businesses and drove policy change. His understanding of Indian shopping/commerce behaviours and ability to balance safety with business impact is relevant. However, this requires SQL/data analytics depth and is more T&S-focused than product/strategy.
how_to_win: Lead with the SMB over-enforcement story — directly analogous to shopping T&S challenges (false positives harming legitimate merchants). Discuss how Indian shopping behaviours create unique T&S challenges that global models miss. Frame data analysis skills as AI-augmented (uses AI tools for data analysis). Position as someone who brings merchant/user empathy to T&S strategy.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 4 years data analytics, Trust and Safety, policy, cybersecurity, or related
• 4 years data analysis with databases and querying (SQL, MySQL)

title: Account Manager, Consumer Package Goods, APAC LCS
role_summary: Account manager for CPG (consumer packaged goods) advertisers in APAC, managing large customer relationships and driving advertising revenue.
why_good_fit: CPG advertising in India — Jay understands the Indian advertising ecosystem from Meta (SMB advertiser conversion, Shoppertainment, Paid Messaging). His understanding of Indian consumer behaviour across segments (GenZ, Tier 2/3, regional) is valuable for CPG advertisers. However, this is an account management/sales role, not product, and requires advertising sales experience.
how_to_win: Frame Meta advertising work as relevant: understanding advertiser needs, SMB-to-advertiser conversion, India revenue growth. Discuss Indian consumer insights that CPG advertisers need — GenZ behaviours, regional consumption patterns, Tier 2/3 market dynamics. Position as someone who brings consumer insight depth to advertiser relationships. This is a pivot role — only pursue if strongly interested in advertising sales.
key_requirements:
• Bachelor's degree
• 5 years advertising sales, marketing, consulting, media, or agency experience
